Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: The current model has the following structure 1 SEQUENCE Create Requisition Approve Requisition PARALLEL BLOCK Close Case 2 PARALLEL BLOCK contains Create PO Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice Pay Invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The parallel block allows activities to happen in any order which violates the domain logic

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: You cannot pay the invoice before receiving it You cannot match the invoice before receiving it You cannot match the invoice before creating the PO You cannot pay the invoice before matching it You cannot receive goods before creating the PO

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The correct sequence should be Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice Close Case

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: The corrected model should be a simple SEQUENCE of all activities because they must occur in order

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: In the Procure to Pay process the requisition and approval must always precede the purchase order the purchase order must be created before goods can be received invoice receipt typically happens after goods are received though sometimes simultaneously matching is only possible once both the goods receipt and invoice exist payment cannot occur until the matching step is complete the case closes after payment

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Some processes allow the invoice to arrive before or after goods receipt but the strict domain logic typically requires goods to be received first

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For this exercise I will implement the most restrictive sequence to prevent any underfitting

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The fixed version uses a straightforward sequence Create Purchase Requisition then Approve Purchase Requisition Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice and Close Case This ensures each step follows the previous one in the correct order eliminating the possibility of unauthorized parallel execution or out of sequence events

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: I am reconsidering whether invoice receipt should occur after goods receipt In Procure to Pay workflows goods and invoices can arrive in either sequence though goods typically come first

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The critical constraint is that matching cannot happen until both have arrived and payment requires successful matching For this strict scenario I will maintain the sequential approach to enforce maximum control
